name: Service Registry and Circuit Derivation
description: Pure functions that load service manifests from directories and derive CircuitSpec configurations by assigning ports, resolving dependencies, and creating node/edge specifications for service orchestration.

types.name: ServiceManifest
types.description: External type from baton.schemas representing a service manifest with name, port, dependencies, proxy_mode, role, metadata, mock_spec, and api_spec fields

types.name: CircuitSpec
types.description: External type from baton.schemas representing a circuit specification with name, version, nodes, and edges

types.name: NodeSpec
types.description: External type from baton.schemas representing a node in the circuit with name, port, proxy_mode, contract, role, and metadata

types.name: EdgeSpec
types.description: External type from baton.schemas representing an edge in the circuit with source, target, and label

functions.name: load_manifests
functions.description: Load manifests from multiple service directories and validate that all service names are unique

inputs.name: service_dirs
inputs.description: List of directory paths (as strings or Path objects) containing service manifests

error_cases.name: duplicate_service_names
error_cases.condition: Two or more manifests have the same service name

error_cases.name: manifest_load_failure
error_cases.condition: load_manifest raises an exception for any directory

postconditions: All service names in returned manifests are unique
Length of returned list equals length of service_dirs input
side_effects: Reads manifest files from each directory via load_manifest

structured_side_effects.target: service manifest files in provided directories

functions.name: derive_circuit
functions.description: Derive a CircuitSpec from a list of service manifests by creating nodes with auto-assigned ports, resolving dependencies, and creating edges

inputs.name: manifests
inputs.description: List of service manifests to derive circuit from

inputs.name: circuit_name
inputs.description: Name to assign to the derived circuit

error_cases.name: port_conflict
error_cases.condition: Two or more manifests specify the same non-zero port

error_cases.name: missing_required_dependency
error_cases.condition: A manifest has a required (non-optional) dependency on a service name not in the manifests list

error_data.message: Service '{m.name}' depends on '{dep.name}' which is not registered

error_cases.name: no_available_ports
error_cases.condition: All ports in the range DEFAULT_PORT_START to DEFAULT_PORT_MAX are already used when trying to auto-assign a port

postconditions: Each manifest results in exactly one NodeSpec in the circuit
All nodes have unique ports assigned
Ports are assigned from DEFAULT_PORT_START to DEFAULT_PORT_MAX range when manifest.port == 0
Each non-optional dependency that exists creates an EdgeSpec from consumer to provider
Optional dependencies that are missing are silently skipped

functions.name: _next_port
functions.description: Find the next available port in the default range (DEFAULT_PORT_START to DEFAULT_PORT_MAX) that is not in the used set

inputs.name: used
inputs.description: Set of port numbers already in use

error_cases.name: no_available_ports
error_cases.condition: All ports in the range DEFAULT_PORT_START to DEFAULT_PORT_MAX are already in the used set

postconditions: Returned port is in range [DEFAULT_PORT_START, DEFAULT_PORT_MAX]
Returned port is not in the used set

invariants: DEFAULT_PORT_START and DEFAULT_PORT_MAX define the valid port range for auto-assignment
All functions are pure except load_manifests which reads from filesystem
Service names must be unique across all loaded manifests
Ports must be unique across all nodes in a circuit
CircuitSpec version is always set to 1
